name: Our stay · Coastal Rose Garden
address: Bldg 2A, Coastal Rose Garden, 3 Jinshiji Rd, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Home base in Shekou. Pin is approximate within the complex; nudge me to set it exact.

name: Eyes of the GBA (Bay Area Book City)
desc: World's largest bookstore: 300,000 books, art gardens, themed halls and AI guides. By Baohua metro (Line 5).
notes: OSM pin vs. the Baohua-station address differ slightly; verify on arrival.

name: Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum
desc: New Zaha Hadid-designed science museum (opened 2025). Metro Line 6 to Guangming.
notes: Reserve tickets ~8pm Beijing time the night before.

name: Shenzhen Natural History Museum
desc: Brand-new natural history museum in Pingshan, opening on a trial basis around now (late June 2026).
notes: Trial operation from June 30, 2026; official opening is July 28 (after your trip), so expect a limited trial visit, likely reservation-only. Confirm before going.

name: SEG Electronics Market (Huaqiangbei)
desc: The legendary Huaqiangbei electronics megamarket: components, gadgets, repairs, prototyping heaven.

name: K33 Discovery Farm
address: Guangming Happy Garden, Zhenyuan Rd, Guangming
desc: Outdoor family farm: dinosaur zone, petting zoo, deer, flower fields and many free rides for kids.
notes: Base entry effectively free; some rides paid. Confirm hours on Trip.com.

name: Nickelodeon Playtime (OCT Harbour)
address: Bldg 2-2B, Qushui Bay, OCT Harbour, 2008 Binhai Ave, Nanshan
desc: 1,500m² indoor playground over 2.5 floors: PAW Patrol, SpongeBob, TMNT and Dora zones, ball pit, meet-and-greets.

name: UNIQLO Lee Theatre — UTme! custom tee
address: G/F, B1 & B2, Lee Theatre, 99 Percival St, Causeway Bay
desc: UTme! is not offered in-store in Shenzhen, so this is the HK flagship: design and print a custom T-shirt or tote in ~15 min.

name: Our stay · Amare Hotel Jongno
desc: Home base in Jongno, steps from Jongno 3-ga station; Insadong and Gyeongbokgung nearby.

name: Dalmaji Plaza BBQ (달맞이광장)
address: Euljiro 3-ga, Jung-gu (~10 min walk from Myeongdong)
desc: Viral Hipjiro outdoor yajang BBQ: bamboo-firewood salt chicken, lively alley atmosphere.
notes: Pin approximate; use Catchtable app for remote waiting. Closing after midnight shown as 25:00/26:00.

name: Jiaochangwei Beach (较场尾)
desc: The Dapeng Peninsula's only beach village: a long sandy strand backed by 300+ stylish seaside B&Bs. Beach volleyball, kayaking, cycling, sunsets.
notes: Beach is free; village is a paid-stay hub. Swim only in designated zones.

name: Dapeng Fortress / Ancient City (大鹏所城)
address: Nanmen West Rd, Pengcheng, Dapeng, Longgang
desc: A Ming-dynasty garrison fortress founded in 1394, now a well-preserved walled town with stone lanes, generals' mansions and temples. Namesake of Shenzhen's nickname Peng City.
notes: Walled town and streets are free; some historic mansions inside may charge or need a timed reservation.

name: Xichong Beach (西涌)
desc: Shenzhen's longest beach, a roughly 5 km arc of soft sand on the southern tip of Dapeng, with clear water, a lighthouse hike and a certified dark-sky stargazing park.
notes: Xichong now uses a managed-reservation system (advance online booking often required, with visitor quotas). Resorts/camps may charge separately.

name: Xili Fruit Farm — Litchi World (西丽果场)
address: Litchi World, Shahe West Rd (north), Xili, Nanshan
desc: Nanshan's largest pick-your-own lychee orchard (est. 1989) by Xili Reservoir, 40,000+ trees and ~10 varieties. All-you-can-eat picking in the early-summer lychee season.
notes: Best guess for your 'Xiti fruit farms': 西丽果场 / Litchi World. Open only in lychee season (~early June to early July); closed off-season.

name: MINISO (COCO Park flagship)
desc: Large MINISO lifestyle/variety store inside Futian COCO Park: cheap homeware, stationery, cosmetics and licensed-character merch.
notes: Coordinates are the COCO Park mall; MINISO is a unit inside. Confirm the floor at the mall directory.

name: LEGO Store (COCO Park)
desc: Official LEGO certified store inside Futian COCO Park, with build tables, exclusive sets and a Pick-a-Brick wall.
notes: Coordinates are the COCO Park mall; confirm the exact unit/floor on arrival.

name: Shenzhen Book City — Central (中心城)
desc: The flagship Shenzhen Book City on the Futian civic axis (near Lianhuashan), ~42,000 m² with cafes, stationery and event space. A landmark cultural venue.
notes: Metro Line 4 Children's Palace (少年宫), exits C2/D. Other Book City branches exist (Luohu, Nanshan, Longgang).

name: Dongmen Street food market (东门老街)
desc: Shenzhen's busiest old-town district: a maze of pedestrian lanes packed with street-food stalls, skewers, desserts, milk tea and cheap fashion. Liveliest in the evening.
notes: Open public street zone, not one venue; stalls run ~10:00 to late. Metro Line 1/3 Laojie (老街).

name: One Avenue 卓悦中心
desc: Upscale CBD mall straddling Fuhua Road, with a big open-air rooftop garden deck, international fashion, dining and an observation level. Linked to the Gangxia metro hub.
notes: Metro Lines 1/3/4 around Gangxia / 会展中心.

name: Longgang Wanda Plaza 龙岗万达广场
desc: China's first 4th-generation Wanda Plaza (2021): an 8-floor complex with the world's longest indoor escalator (~64 m), a 2,500 m² LED ceiling and a glass skywalk. A viral destination.
notes: Shenzhen has several Wanda Plazas (e.g. Guangming ~22.7515,113.9278, plus Longhua). This is the landmark one but sits in the far NE (Metro Line 10, Mugu). Say the word to swap for a more central branch.

name: Nantou 1820 Digital Exhibition Hall (南头古城数字展厅)
address: By Baode Square, Nantou Ancient City, Nanshan
desc: A small multimedia museum inside Nantou Ancient City named for the city's 1820 Qing-dynasty heyday. A 360° immersive animation of the silk scroll recreates trade and daily life of 200 years ago.
notes: Closed Mondays. Coords are the Nantou Ancient City entrance; the hall is a short walk inside the walls. Photos show the old city, not the hall interior.

name: Shenzhen Safari Park (深圳野生动物园)
desc: Large drive/walk-through wildlife park on Xili Lake with free-ranging animals, a safari zone and daily animal shows. One of Shenzhen's oldest family attractions.
notes: Last entry ~17:00.

name: Splendid China Folk Culture Village (锦绣中华)
desc: Combined theme park: a miniature-landmarks park plus a folk-culture village of China's ethnic groups, with nightly song-and-dance shows. Plan a full day.

price.note: ~100-225 by session; evening cheaper; kids <1.2m free

notes: Night admission from ~17:30. Metro Line 1 Huaqiaocheng / OCT.

name: Bao'an Happy Harbour & Bay Glory wheel (宝安欢乐港湾)
desc: Waterfront leisure district around the 128m Bay Glory Ferris wheel, with a daily music-fountain and light show on the central plaza. The harbour is free to enter.
notes: Light/water show 20:00 weekdays; 20:00 & 21:00 weekends/holidays, ~15 min, daily except Mondays. Wheel ticket ~¥118. Fireworks only on special event nights.

name: Qianhai Huafa Ice & Snow World (前海·华发冰雪世界)
desc: World's largest indoor ski resort (Guinness), ~100,000 m² kept near -6°C, with 5 slopes up to 463m and an 83m drop, plus a ski-in/ski-out JW Marriott. Opened Sep 2025.
notes: Newest large indoor ski resort. Site is in Bao'an near Qianhai Bay. Confirm daily session times with the official channel.

name: MOCAPE (当代艺术与城市规划馆)
desc: Striking Coop Himmelb(l)au-designed building combining a contemporary-art museum and a city-planning exhibition hall under one roof. Opened 2016.
notes: Closed Mondays; last admission 17:00. Free general admission. Metro Line 3/4 Children's Palace, Exit A2.

name: Dafen Oil Painting Village (大芬油画村)
desc: Dense art village with 1,000+ studios and galleries famous for hand-painted oil reproductions and originals. Free to wander; try a painting class.
notes: Most shops 10:00-22:00. Dafen Art Museum free, Tue-Sun 09:30-17:30. Metro Line 3 Dafen, Exit A1.

name: WonderMeet (玩美攻略)
address: Century Plaza, 3018 Shennan Middle Rd, Futian
desc: Huge three-floor indoor amusement complex with one all-day ticket covering 100+ attractions: karting, bowling, ice skating, VR, 5D theatre, laser tag, bumper cars and more.
notes: Floors 1F/2F/3F of Century Plaza. Metro Line 1/7 Huaqiang Rd, Exit B.

name: KP-FUN Trendy Play Street (AR karting)
address: 3F, C Future City, 9283 Binhe Ave, Futian
desc: New two-floor indoor entertainment street (opened June 2025) headlined by AR/MR go-karting (you wear mixed-reality goggles), plus a 360° rotating motorbike, a big bumper-car arena and 100+ tech attractions.
notes: The AR-goggle (MR) karting venue. Coords approximate, by Metro Line 9 Xiasha, Exit B. Track-style alternative without AR: SGP Karting Club, Luohu.

name: Sushiro 寿司郎 (Coastal City / 海岸城)
address: 3F, Coastal City, 33 Wenxin 5th Rd, Houhai, Nanshan
desc: Japanese conveyor-belt sushi chain. The Coastal City outlet in Houhai is the branch inside Nanshan. Hugely popular, long queues.
notes: Only Sushiro within Nanshan. No Shekou branch; others are in Futian (卓悦中心, 皇庭广场) and Longgang.

name: Haidilao 海底捞 (Baoneng Taikoo City)
address: NL309-310, Baoneng Taikoo City, 2233 Zhongxin Rd, Nanshan
desc: The Haidilao closest to Shekou, in Baoneng Taikoo City by Shenzhen Bay. Famous for service, free snacks and late hours.
notes: Late-night closing times approximate.

name: Haidilao 海底捞 (Hanjing Center)
address: 3F, Hanjing Center, 23 Dengliang Rd, Houhai, Nanshan
desc: Second-nearest Haidilao to Shekou, in the Houhai / Shenzhen Bay area. Same hot pot, tableside service and late hours.
notes: Coordinates approximate (Hanjing Center, Houhai).

name: Nong Geng Ji 农耕记 (Sea World, Michelin)
address: Unit 303, 3F Zone B, Sea World Plaza, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Shenzhen-born Hunan country-style (湖南土菜) chain recognised in the Michelin Guide; the Sea World outlet sits by the Shekou waterfront. Rustic, spicy home cooking.
notes: The Shekou Michelin Hunan restaurant is Nong Geng Ji (农耕记), founded in Shenzhen 2017.

name: Mood Coffee (Shekou)
desc: A specialty coffee spot in the Shekou area.
notes: Unverified by name on web/Dianping/OSM; pin is an approximate Shekou placeholder. Confirm exact spot. Nearby verified cafes: SAANCI 山池咖啡, JoJo's Turkish Café, Day By Coffee.

name: G&G Creative Community 创意社区
desc: A former factory turned hip creative community with design shops, cafes, exhibition halls, art markets and a basketball court. ~650 m from Shuiwan Station (Line 2) Exit D.
notes: Coordinates approximate; individual shop hours vary.

name: Nanhai Yiku 南海意库 (OCT Ideapark)
desc: A waterside creative park converted from a 1980s factory into six garden buildings with 120+ design firms, cafes, shops and industrial-chic photo spots. 2-3 min from Shuiwan Station Exit D.
notes: Open-access grounds; tenant hours vary.

name: Sea World Music Fountain Show 音乐喷泉
address: Water plaza by the Minghua ship, Sea World, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Shenzhen's largest open-air water show: music, fountains, lights and fire around the historic Minghua cruise ship. ~10 min per show, free to watch from the plaza.
notes: Show slots: Tue-Sun 19:30/20:00/20:30, plus 21:00/21:30 on Fri & Sat. Closed Monday. Times shift for weather/holidays.

name: Dental Bauhinia 紫荆口腔 (Shekou)
address: Shop 27, Phase II, Coastal Rose Garden, Wanghai Rd, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Established international, English-speaking dental clinic in Shekou with dentists from HK, Australia, Canada and the mainland. General, cosmetic, orthodontic and implant care.
notes: No clinic named exactly 'Mayia' could be verified in Shekou; this is the substituted best-known English-speaking Shekou clinic. Coords approximate; confirm hours.

name: Zhongshuge Bookstore 钟书阁
address: East Bank L1-021, Happy Coast (欢乐港湾), Bao'an
desc: Shenzhen's branch of the famously photogenic 'most beautiful bookstore in China', with mirrored ceilings, spiral shelves and a tiered reading staircase.
notes: In Happy Coast East Bank L1-021. Metro Linhai (Line 5/12) Exit B2.

name: UpperHills 深业上城
desc: Mixed-use mall and LOFT complex backing onto Lianhua Hill Park, with an open-air European-style 'Town' street of design boutiques and cafes on the L3 podium.
notes: Adjacent to Lianhua Hill Park.

name: 24 Histories Academy 二十四史书院
desc: An Oriental-garden cultural academy and bookstore themed on China's Twenty-Four Histories, with themed bookshops, a children's picture-book room, tea gatherings and evening folk music.
notes: In Gankeng Ancient Town; Metro Gankeng (Line 10) Exit B.

name: Hongqiao Park 虹桥公园 (red skywalk)
desc: A hillside forest park famous for its ~4 km elevated bright-red 'rainbow' skywalk winding through the treetops, a major photo spot.
notes: This is 虹桥公园 in Guangming. If you meant the central 洪湖公园 in Luohu (lotus ponds, also a red bridge), tell me and I'll switch it.

name: Shenzhen Bay Culture Square 深圳湾文化广场
desc: A major new 188,000 m² design-and-culture complex on Shenzhen Bay by MAD Architects (Ma Yansong), with design-art exhibition halls and cultural spaces. Opened Nov 2025.
notes: Last entry 17:00. Monday closure unconfirmed; ticket price TBC. Metro Line 13 Talent Park Exit B1.

name: Shiopon (salt-bread bakery)
address: L1 CL120, Shenzhen Bay MixC (深圳湾万象城), Nanshan
desc: Japanese-style salt-bread (盐面包) bakery, crisp outside and soft inside in many flavours. Often long queues.
notes: In Shenzhen Bay MixC; coords are mall-level.

name: Ah Ma Handmade 阿嬷手作
address: L4 SL432, The MixC World (万象天地), Nanshan
desc: Popular Guangxi handmade dessert and drinks chain, known for taro (芋泥) and fresh sugar-cane drinks. The branch nearest Science Park, Nanshan.
notes: 10+ Shenzhen branches; also at One Avenue, 金光华, 欢乐港湾. Coords mall-level.

name: GIF Coffee 集福咖啡
address: A106, Bldg 541, Bagualing Industrial Zone, Futian
desc: Well-regarded specialty coffee shop in the Bagualing area, run by a couple (the barista was a 2020 China-competition runner-up). GIF = 'Get Interesting Food'.
notes: In Futian (Bagualing), not Shekou. Coords block-level approximate.

name: Yuangu 元古云境
address: NL115A, The Lane, MixC World (万象天地), Nanshan
desc: Stylish neo-Chinese restaurant and tea house with creative contemporary cuisine and a Zen/afternoon-tea aesthetic, in the open-air lane of MixC World.
notes: Coords mall-level. A second branch is at L235, Shenzhen Bay MixC.

name: Spring Patio 春台餐酒馆
address: 2F open-air corridor, MixC World (万象天地), Nanshan
desc: Upscale Yunnan-cuisine restaurant and bar on the 2nd-floor open-air corridor of MixC World, known for its award-winning interior.
notes: Coords mall-level.

name: The Bay by Chef Fei 东湾
address: 1F, Mandarin Oriental, Block A UpperHills, 5001 Huanggang Rd, Futian
desc: Refined Cantonese restaurant at the Mandarin Oriental, led by two-Michelin-star Chef Fei. One Diamond in the 2026 Black Pearl Guide.
notes: Upscale; reservations recommended.

name: Sheng Xiang Ting 盛香亭 (Happy Harbour)
address: B1 food hall (B1北-080-081), Bao'an Happy Harbour East Bank
desc: Changsha-style hot-braise (热卤) Hunan rice noodles, Angus beef noodles and snacks. The well-rated Hunan noodle spot inside Bao'an Happy Harbour.
notes: Inside Happy Harbour B1; coords mall-level. Sit-down Hunan alternative in the same mall: 农耕记 (2F).

name: Shiheyuan 柿合缘 新京菜
address: L2 NL219, MixC World (万象天地), Nanshan
desc: Upscale 'new Beijing cuisine' restaurant (Michelin / Black Pearl recognised) in MixC World, known for three-way Peking duck and private dining rooms.
notes: Best match for the name you gave ('Shieyuan') is 柿合缘 (Shi-he-yuan). Coords mall-level (unit L2).

name: Sea World Culture & Arts Center (Design Society)
desc: Fumihiko Maki-designed waterfront cultural complex in Shekou, home to Design Society and the V&A Gallery (the V&A's first international collaboration). A landmark for design and contemporary art on Shenzhen Bay.
notes: Closed Mondays. Public spaces free; some V&A and special exhibitions are ticketed. Walkable from your Shekou base.

name: Shenzhen Art Museum (New)
desc: The flagship new home of Shenzhen Art Museum (opened 2023) with vast modern galleries for Chinese ink, contemporary and international art. One of the city's largest art venues.
notes: Closed Mondays. Free; online reservation recommended.

name: He Xiangning Art Museum
desc: Opened 1997, China's first national art museum named after an individual artist (revolutionary painter He Xiangning). Her ink works plus a strong contemporary program, linked to the OCAT terminal nearby.
notes: Closed Mondays. Free.

name: Guan Shanyue Art Museum 关山月美术馆
desc: Dedicated to Lingnan-school master Guan Shanyue. A leading venue for traditional Chinese ink painting alongside design and contemporary shows, beside Lianhua Hill Park.
notes: Closed Mondays. Free.

name: Shenzhen Museum (History & Folk Culture)
desc: The main history and folk-culture wing of Shenzhen Museum in the Civic Center, tracing the city's leap from fishing villages to a global tech metropolis. The best overview of Shenzhen's story.
notes: Closed Mondays. Free; bring your passport for entry.

name: Aqila Hot Spring Spa 阿奇拉汤泉生活
address: Floors 2-3, Tianxia Jinniu Plaza, Taoyuan Rd, Nanshan
desc: Large 24-hour upscale hot-spring bathhouse and leisure complex: hot pools, saunas, plus a cinema, arcade/VR, consoles, esports cafe, billiards, karaoke, hanfu area and an indoor diving pool. Unlimited fruit and drinks included.

price.note: approx 24h bath ticket; deals from ~55% off via Klook/Trip.com

notes: 24h, near Taoyuan station (Line 1/12). Free shuttle to/from Shenzhen Bay Port. Coords adjusted from Amap to approximate WGS84.

name: Maya Beach Water Park 玛雅海滩水公园
desc: Shenzhen's most famous water park, inside Happy Valley OCT: 30+ attractions including a 15m Rainbow Racer, a water coaster, wave pool, lazy river and the Tikal kids zone. Hosts the Maya electronic-music season in July-August.

price.note: approx; time-based; day ticket covers Happy Valley too

notes: Water-park season runs roughly June-September; July is peak (often 10:00-22:00).

name: Dameisha Seaside Park 大梅沙海滨公园
desc: Shenzhen's longest free public beach (1,800m): soft sand, gentle swimming areas, water sports and seaside playgrounds. The adjacent OCT East adds a dedicated water park.
notes: Free but peak-summer online reservation usually required. Swim season ~April-October.

name: Guanlan Shanshui Eco Water Park 观澜山水田园
desc: Family eco resort with a seasonal outdoor water park: a tornado funnel slide, rainbow racer and 10+ water attractions in a garden setting, plus on-site hot springs. A lower-key alternative to the big parks.
notes: Water park seasonal May-October; July in season.

name: Xinggaodu Dream Water World 星高度梦想水世界
address: Yunli Dream Park, Fada Rd, Bantian, Longgang
desc: A 30,000 m² pink-themed outdoor water world with flamingo, dolphin-cove and unicorn-castle zones, geared to family photo-fun rather than extreme thrills. Metro-accessible and budget-priced.
notes: ~150m from Metro Line 5 Bantian Exit D. Seasonal summer; bring a swim ring.

name: Haibei Bay 海贝湾 (Shenzhen's Santorini)
desc: A cliff-backed cove nicknamed 'Shenzhen's Santorini' for its cluster of white-and-blue Greek-style villas and cliff-edge restaurants (the Miyue Santorini resort, Positano cliff dining) above the beach. A small cliff lift reportedly runs down to the sea.
notes: Best match for the Greek/Santorini look with restaurants, and the cliff-lift matches your memory (not fully confirmed online). Coords approximate (Nan'ao); phone is the resort's. The bigger Kaisa Golden Bay nearby is themed on local fishing culture, not Greek.

name: Changhwadang 창화당 (Hannam)
desc: Hotteok and Korean street-food snacks.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Niuroumianguan (beef noodle)
desc: Beef noodle soup.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Kyochon Pilbang
desc: Korean fried chicken.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Bukchon Son-mandu
desc: Handmade dumplings near Insadong.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Wonjo Nude Cheese Kimbap
desc: Cheese kimbap.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Jinmi Sikdang
desc: Korean restaurant.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Noryangjin Fisheries Market
desc: Huge seafood wholesale market; pick and have it cooked.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Isaac Toast (Myeongdong Cathedral)
desc: Griddled toast sandwiches and coffee.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Gwangjang Market
desc: Famous traditional market and street-food hall (bindaetteok, mayak gimbap).
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: BukChangDong Soondubu (Myeongdong)
desc: Soft tofu stew (soondubu).
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Eunjujeong
desc: Korean restaurant.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Hanilgwan Gwanghwamun
desc: Long-running traditional Korean restaurant.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Namsandong Gotgan 남산동곳간
desc: Korean restaurant.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Golden Farm
desc: Myeongdong eatery.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Yell-o! Chicken
desc: Fried chicken, Itaewon.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Hanndul Chicken
desc: Fried chicken.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Dailybeer 생활맥주 (Myeongdong)
desc: Craft beer pub.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Kkanbu Chicken (Myeongdong)
desc: Fried chicken.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: BBQ Chicken Myeongdong Star
desc: Korean fried chicken.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Kyochon Chicken (Dongdaemun)
desc: Fried chicken.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Korean Emperor Buffet
desc: Korean BBQ buffet.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: AYCE Pig Company (Myeongdong BBQ)
desc: All-you-can-eat Korean BBQ.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Myeongdong Mongvely
desc: Myeongdong eatery.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: ZIU BBQ
desc: Korean BBQ.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Wolhwa Sikdang
desc: Korean restaurant.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Daol Charcoal BBQ
desc: Charcoal Korean BBQ.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Bogwangjung
desc: Restaurant near Itaewon.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Shuiwan 1979 Plaza 水湾1979
desc: Creative dining-and-shopping plaza in Shekou with restored old buildings.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Café de Coral (Admiralty)
desc: Classic Hong Kong fast-food chain.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Yuen Po Bird Market
address: Flower Market Rd, Mong Kok, Hong Kong
desc: Bird garden and market with songbirds and ornate cages.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Cat Street (Upper Lascar Row)
address: Upper Lascar Row, Sheung Wan, Hong Kong
desc: Antiques, curios and bric-a-brac street.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Sham Shui Po Toy Street
address: Fuk Wing St, Sham Shui Po, Hong Kong
desc: Street lined with toy and gadget wholesalers.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Graham Street Market
address: Gage St / Graham St, Central, Hong Kong
desc: Historic open-air wet market in Central.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Tai Yuen Toy Street (Wan Chai)
address: Tai Yuen St, Wan Chai, Hong Kong
desc: Old-school toy and market street.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Hong Kong Maritime Museum
desc: Harbourfront museum on HK's maritime history.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: HK Zoological & Botanical Gardens
desc: Historic gardens with greenhouses, birds and primates.
notes: From your Google Maps 'Want to go' list.

name: Peticano 毕加诺
desc: Argentine/American wood-fired smokehouse (ribs, brisket, steaks) with a buzzy terrace. A long-running Shekou institution.
commute: ~5 min walk from Sea World station (Line 2/12), in Shekou.
notes: Dianping ~4.6. Expat favorite.

name: 1-7 Bread 壹柒面包 (Sea World)
desc: Artisan small-batch sourdough and low-sugar bakes (cod-roe baguette, milk toast), rated among Nanshan's best bread.
commute: ~3 min walk, inside Sea World.
notes: Dianping ~4.7.

name: Dananshan Park 大南山公园
desc: A 336 m granite-step climb from Shekou to a beacon-tower summit with sweeping views over Shenzhen Bay, the skyline and Hong Kong. Best at sunset.
commute: ~10 min walk/DiDi to the Minghua trailhead from Shekou.
notes: Amap 4.7. Top sunset viewpoint.

name: Mekong River Sports Town 湄南河体育小镇
desc: A 25,000 m² indoor sports park with 30+ activities: a real indoor ski slope, karting, trampolines, climbing and surf. A rainy-day winner for the family.
commute: ~6 min DiDi from Shekou / Sea World.
notes: Dianping ~4.6.

name: Long Bar (Raffles, One Shenzhen Bay)
address: Raffles, One Shenzhen Bay, Houhai, Nanshan
desc: Elegant high-floor hotel cocktail bar with floor-to-ceiling views over Shenzhen Bay and the bridge. The area's most polished sundowner.
commute: ~10 min DiDi from Shekou (or Line 2 to Houhai).
notes: At Raffles / One Shenzhen Bay.

name: 24気 Art & Life Space 24气艺文生活空间
desc: Glass-walled art-and-coffee space framing Houhai Bay and the Shenzhen Bay bridge, known for its rosemary americano.
commute: ~12 min DiDi from Shekou.
notes: Dianping ~4.6. Bay-view design cafe.

name: Fukanglou Coffee 福康楼咖啡
desc: Teahouse-meets-cafe serving inventive Chinese specialty lattes (osmanthus-rice, black-sesame) in lidded gaiwan bowls.
commute: ~15 min via Line 2 to Houhai / Coastal City.
notes: Dianping ~4.7.

name: Crab Xingji 蟹兴记
desc: Shenzhen's only branch of this crab specialist, hand-picking fresh crab daily for hairy-crab roe dishes. A refined seafood splurge.
commute: ~15 min via Line 2 to Houhai.
notes: Dianping 4.7, on the 必吃榜 Must-Eat list.

name: Nanshan Museum 南山博物馆
desc: A big, free, well-curated museum with a Nanshan-history wing plus rotating blockbuster shows (e.g. Egyptian golden mummies). Spacious and English-friendly.
commute: ~20 min DiDi, or Line 2 then Line 1 to 桃园 (Exit B).
notes: Amap ~4.8. Free; likely closed Mondays.

name: Peng Xiao Guan 鹏小馆 (OCT)
desc: Modern Cantonese darling famous for its clay-pot 'kiln chicken' (窑鸡) and inventive Guangdong small plates. A reliable must-eat near OCT-LOFT.
commute: ~22 min via Line 2 to OCT (侨城东).
notes: Dianping 必吃榜.

name: OCT-LOFT 华侨城创意文化园
desc: A converted-factory creative district of ~300 design studios, the OCAT contemporary-art galleries, Old Heaven indie bookshop/music venue, and the weekend T-Street creative market.
commute: ~25 min via Line 2 to 侨城东 / Line 1 侨城东.
notes: Amap ~4.6. Shenzhen's top creative park.

name: Batiaoyou 八条友烧烤 (Nanyou flagship)
desc: Open-front Nanyou skewer stall: fresh-cut charcoal skewers (lamb, beef tendon, chicken wings, leeks) plus the chain's signature crispy grilled fish (脆皮烤鱼) and cheap late-night beer. Plastic stools, smoky street vibe, busy after dark.
commute: ~10 min DiDi from Shekou (Nanyou), or Line 2/12 toward 南山 then a short walk.
notes: Closest skewer match to Shekou (~2.8 km). Name caveat: Dianping lists it as 八条友烧烤·脆皮烤鱼 (南山旗舰店), but Amap/Meituan show the near-identical sister brand 六条友烧烤 at this spot, possibly a renamed/copycat store. Same concept.

name: Batiaoyou 八条友烧烤 (Bao'an BREWTOWN)
address: Snow BREWTOWN Beer Town, EL134, Xin'an, Bao'an
desc: A confirmed 八条友烧烤·脆皮烤鱼 outlet inside the BREWTOWN beer-town complex: charcoal-grilled skewers and crispy grilled fish paired with Snow/craft beer. Casual food-court-meets-night-market setting.
commute: ~25-35 min DiDi from Shekou (Bao'an Xin'an); borderline within 30 min.
notes: Nearest genuine 八条友 brand branch to Shekou (~10.8 km). Other branches (Futian, 石岩, 松岗, 观澜, Guangming) are all 15 km+ and over 30 min.

name: K11 ECOAST 深圳K11 ECOAST
address: Taizi Bay / Prince Bay, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Brand-new (Apr 2025) OMA / Sou Fujimoto seafront art-mall with a Promenade boardwalk and flagship stores. The buzziest new opening on the Shekou waterfront, a citywalk favorite for the architecture and sea views.
commute: ~8 min DiDi from Shekou (Taizi Bay), or a waterfront walk.
notes: Featured by SCMP Style, Time Out, EyeShenzhen.

name: Innate Ayurveda Spa 印度阿育吠陀
address: Seaside Commercial Center, Sea World, Shekou
desc: Authentic Kerala Ayurveda (Abhyanga oil massage, Shirodhara) right in Sea World. A calmer boutique alternative to the mega-spas that HK visitors cross the border for.
commute: ~5 min walk, in the Sea World commercial center.
notes: Featured by Sassy Hong Kong and Cathay Pacific 'best Shenzhen spas'.

name: Houhaihui 后海汇
desc: A Gen-Z trend mall with vintage shops, art-toy/blind-box stores, a climbing gym, rooftop garden and weekend night markets. A top Xiaohongshu citywalk stop for HK youngsters.
commute: ~18 min: Line 2 to 后海 + short walk, or DiDi ~15 min.
notes: Xiaohongshu / Trip.com HK 'trendy destination'.

name: Talent Park 人才公园
desc: A lakeside bayfront park where you can jog, citywalk and shoot the skyline. The headline 'see Hong Kong from the other side' viewpoint, with weekend evening light shows.
commute: ~15 min DiDi from Shekou, or Line 2 to 后海/登良 + walk.
notes: Top citywalk pick in HK Xiaohongshu '深圳周末' guides. Its light show is also in the Events tab.

name: Bollywood Cafe 宝莱坞印度餐厅
desc: A well-rated authentic Indian curry house near Houhai that HK foodies single out when they want something beyond hotpot on a Shenzhen weekend.
commute: ~20 min DiDi from Shekou (Houhai).
notes: Featured in Sassy Hong Kong 'Ultimate Dining Spots in Shenzhen'.

name: Xie San Bao 蟹三宝
desc: Cheap-and-cheerful crab-roe (蟹黄) noodle and rice bowls. An Instagrammable budget bite HK foodies queue for, exactly the 'great value vs HK' draw of crossing the border.
commute: ~22 min DiDi from Shekou (Nanshan).
notes: Featured in Sassy Hong Kong Shenzhen dining guide.

name: Yewai Urban Tea Room 叶外·Urban茶室
address: Coastal City / Haian Cheng, Houhai, Nanshan
desc: A photogenic 'new-Chinese' tea-and-dessert room at Coastal City that HK media flags as a must-photo trendy stop on a Houhai food day.
commute: ~20 min via Line 2 to 后海 / Coastal City.
notes: Highlighted in U Travel HK '深圳海岸城' guide.

name: Nantou Ancient City maker district 南头古城主理人街区
desc: The restored Nantou Ancient City packs 280+ independent 主理人 design brands and studios (original fashion, bags, jewelry, leather, vintage like Soarin) plus recurring weekend maker markets of 100+ stalls. Shenzhen's closest equivalent to Hong Kong's PMQ.
commute: ~15 min DiDi from Shekou, or Line 12 to 南头古城.
notes: Independent-designer shopping. Maker/民艺 markets run on weekends; shops generally open daily ~11:00-21:00. Distinct from the small 1820 digital hall pin nearby.

name: Shekou Seafood Market 蛇口海鲜市场
desc: Classic Shekou pick-and-cook seafood market: buy live fish, prawns, crab and geoduck by weight from the wet-market stalls, then hand it to the adjacent 加工 restaurants who cook it to order for a processing fee. Stocked daily from the Shekou fishing harbour.

price.note: seafood by weight + processing fee ~¥10-20 per 斤

commute: ~5 min DiDi or ~12 min walk from the apartment (~1 km); Metro Dongjiaotou (Line 2/8/12) ~230 m.
notes: Pick downstairs, cooked at the adjacent 加工 eateries (e.g. 德记烧腊饭店) for a per-斤 fee. Processing stalls are next-door rather than strictly upstairs.

name: Bell Nature Exploration Center 贝尔自然探索中心
address: Sea World Zone D / Bow Plaza, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Indoor nature center with three halls: a Pet Park where kids feed and pet alpacas, rabbits, guinea pigs, marmots, a mini pony, ducks, tortoises and parrots; an ocean/marine-touch hall; and a hands-on explorer hall, plus animal shows.
commute: ~10 min from Shekou, walkable from the apartment (Sea World, Line 2 exit A).
notes: 4yo: excellent (gentle animals, ball pit, shows); 12yo: the explorer + ocean halls and bigger animals keep him engaged. Indoor/weatherproof. Dianping/Ctrip ~4.7. Weekends to 21:00.

name: Meland Club (MixC World) 美兰湖儿童乐园
address: 3F SL382, MixC World 万象天地, 9668 Shennan Blvd, Nanshan
desc: ~3000 m² flagship soft-play club: an 8.5m multi-level climbing maze, trampolines, big ball pits, mini-bowling and a 'Mini City' role-play zone (firefighter/chef/doctor). One of the best-regarded indoor play clubs in Nanshan.
commute: Metro ~35 min: Line 12 direct to 桃园, change Line 1 to 高新园 (Exit A, 480 m). DiDi ~18 min, ¥30. DiDi is faster.
notes: 4yo: excellent (huge varied soft-play, role-play, trampolines); 12yo: can do the tall climbing maze and trampolines but he's at the top of its age cap (max 1.5m), may feel young. Mon is deep-clean (shorter hours). Dianping ~4.7.

name: Wooderful Land 木育森林
address: L2, Yitian Holiday Plaza 益田假日广场, OCT, Nanshan
desc: Calm, design-led wooden play forest: wooden slides, gentle climbing structures, marble runs and hands-on wooden toys. Clean and developmental, ideal for a 4yo who finds big ball-pit parks overwhelming.
commute: Direct Line 2 from Sea World to 世界之窗 (5 min walk to 益田假日), ~30 min, no transfer. DiDi ~16 min, ¥28. Take the direct metro.
notes: 4yo: great (gentle wooden play, fine-motor toys); 12yo: too young, no active/thrill activities, he'd be bored. Dianping ~4.6.

name: Di Si Yuan Su Family Center 第肆元素家庭娱乐中心
address: G floor atrium, O'PLAZA, OCT Harbour, Nanshan
desc: ~900 m² soft-play center with an ocean ball pit, trampolines, slides and a sand-play area, sold as unlimited single-day play. Big open soft-play a 4yo can roam for hours.
commute: Metro ~35 min (Line 2 → 后海 → Line 11 红树湾南 → Line 9 深圳湾公园, 2 transfers). DiDi ~16 min, ¥26. Recommend DiDi.
notes: 4yo: good (ball pit, small trampolines, sand, slides); 12yo: toddler/preschool soft-play, too young for him. Dianping well-reviewed.

name: Skytime Trampoline Park 天时蹦床公园
address: 2 Qiongyu Rd, Science & Technology Park, Nanshan
desc: ~1500 m² trampoline park with a free-jump zone, foam/sponge pit, rock-climbing wall, balance ropes and dodgeball. The best of this list for the older sibling.
commute: Metro ~35 min: Line 12 to 桃园, change Line 1 to 深大. DiDi ~15 min, ¥26. Short hop, DiDi is faster.
notes: 4yo: limited (foam pit/free-jump with a parent); 12yo: excellent (trampolines, rock climbing, dodgeball, balance rope). Trip.com 4.4. Bring grip socks.

name: Pengcheng International Chess Club 鹏城国际象棋俱乐部
address: 5F-503 Juooot Theatre, Nanshan Cultural & Sports Center, Nanshan Ave
desc: One of Shenzhen's oldest and largest 国际象棋 (Western chess) clubs (founded 2005), with a training center inside the Nanshan Cultural & Sports Center. Holds weekend classes and intra-club practice/rated games.
commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 桃园 (Exit B, 500 m), ~25 min, no transfer. DiDi ~15 min, ¥24. Take the direct metro.
notes: For your 12yo: call to slot him into a weekend session or be matched with strong students/coaches. By appointment, not casual drop-in. Kid-focused with adult coaches. Also mobile 13316915786.

name: Zhi Teng International Chess Club 智腾国际象棋俱乐部
address: Bldg 93 ground floor, Taoyuan Village, Nanshan (next to Dingdangda Bookstore)
desc: A Nanshan junior-focused 国际象棋 academy that runs the area's international-school tournaments (it presented the 2025 ACAMIS Chess Tournament). The best fit to match a ranked 12yo against strong local junior peers.
commute: Metro is a 2-transfer detour (~55 min). DiDi ~25 min, ¥45, direct. Strongly recommend DiDi.
notes: For your 12yo: call He laoshi (何老师) to arrange a practice/junior game, they organise youth competitions and can pair a visiting ranked player. By appointment.

name: Shekou Yucai Library 南山图书馆蛇口育才分馆
desc: The closest public library to the apartment, a small neighbourhood branch of the Nanshan Library system. A quick stop to grab books rather than a destination.
commute: ~10 min from Shekou (within Shekou).
notes: Amenities: book lending + free wifi only; no kids room/cafe/games; limited English books. Foreigners can walk in; bring passport for a borrowing card. Closed Mondays.

name: Nanshan District Library (main) 南山图书馆总馆
address: 176 Changxing Rd, Nanshan (Metro Line 1 Taoyuan, Exit B)
desc: The flagship district library (16,400 m², open 365 days), the best all-round family pick: separate children's and parent-child reading rooms, a large 365-day study hall, a classical-collection room and a regular lecture/event program.
commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 南头古城 (Exit E4, 175 m), ~25 min, no transfer. DiDi ~15 min, ¥24. Take the direct metro.
notes: Amenities: children's room (6-12) + parent-child room (0-6, Mon closed, weekday 15:30-21:00, weekends 09:00-21:00); big study hall; events; some branches have a chess/board-game club; modest English shelf. Free; passport accepted for a card/entry.

name: Nanshan Shufang 24h Smart Reading Rooms 南山书房
address: Design-led smart reading rooms in parks (e.g. Lixiang Park 荔香公园), Nanshan
desc: An award-winning network of unmanned, architecturally striking 24-hour 'reading rooms in parks' with immersive study carrels and full self-service.
commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 桃园 (by 荔香公园), ~25 min, no transfer. DiDi ~14 min, ¥23. Take the direct metro.
notes: Amenities: ~21 immersive study carrels + reading seats; self-service coffee/print/vending; self-checkout; free wifi. Entry by WeChat mini-program '南山书房预约' + face/ID scan, easiest for foreigners after registering a reader card with passport. Mostly Chinese stock.

name: Shenzhen Bay 'Egret Slope' Reading Bar 深圳湾·白鹭坡书吧
address: Egret Slope, Shenzhen Bay Park, Nanshan
desc: A small scenic reading bar on the Shenzhen Bay waterfront, ideal to combine with a family seaside walk. More a cozy reading lounge than a full library.
commute: Metro ~30 min (Line 2 → 后海 → Line 11 to 红树湾南), 1 transfer. DiDi ~15 min, ¥24. DiDi if in a hurry.
notes: Amenities: reading lounge with sea views, free wifi, light refreshments; limited kids room; mostly Chinese books. Free walk-in within the public park; passport only if you want to borrow.

name: lesbobos Aromatherapy SPA 有界时空芳疗SPA (海上世界双玺店)
address: Shuangxi Garden Phase 1, 113-114, Wanghai Rd, Sea World, Shekou
desc: High-end aromatherapy body/oil SPA in private rooms, warm-up (hot sea-salt / bian-stone) before a hands-on massage, with a strict no-upselling policy. The top-rated spa in Nanshan.
commute: ~10 min walk (~750 m) from the apartment.
notes: Dianping 5.0, #1 in Nanshan (15,000+ reviews). Booking recommended; transparent fixed pricing, no upsell.

name: LiSpa Hatsune Japanese Oil Massage LiSPA初音日式精油 (海上世界店)
address: Bitao Garden Villa 10, Gongye 1st Rd, Shekou
desc: Kyoto-style Japanese aromatic-oil full-body massage in a quiet converted villa (head, abdomen, arm and leg meridian work, shower included). Praised for ambience and no hard-sell.

price.note: ~¥312; 70-min oil package often discounted to ¥98-198 on Dianping

commute: ~15 min walk (~1.2 km) from the apartment.
notes: Walk-in possible but booking better (limited villa rooms). Mostly Mandarin.

name: iSpa at Hilton Shekou Nanhai 蛇口希尔顿南海酒店iSpa
address: 3F, Hilton Shenzhen Shekou Nanhai, 1177 Wanghai Rd
desc: Upscale hotel day-spa with sea-view rooms: Thai foot baths, aroma-oil and full-body massage, custom aromatherapy and a tea/snack lounge. The most English-friendly option.
commute: ~8 min walk (~640 m) from the apartment.
notes: Booking recommended; most English-friendly (5-star Hilton).

name: William International Equestrian Center 威廉国际马术中心
address: 4 Haiwan Rd, Value Factory Phase 2, Taizi Bay, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: A 30,000 m² club right in Shekou with cute ponies, medium horses and big warmbloods, plus indoor (45x48m) and outdoor (64x40m) arenas. Does both pony experiences for little kids and proper riding.
commute: ~5-10 min from Shekou.
notes: Ponies for the 4yo and bigger horses for your pony-riding daughter. Trial/experience rides (not membership-only); call to book a 小马 trial + a proper ride. Also +852 5527 8767 (WhatsApp).

name: Banban Meow Cat Cafe 伴伴喵迹海景猫咖 (K11 ECOAST)
address: 8F North Building, K11 ECOAST, 61 Taizi Bay Rd, Shekou
desc: Huge ~5,000 m² ocean-view cat cafe with 100-150 cats of many breeds (Ragdoll, Maine Coon, Sphynx, Devon Rex) plus a small exotic-pet corner and a 180-degree sea-view terrace; ticket includes treats and a drink.

price.note: per person incl. drink; group deals often ~¥128

commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 太子湾, just 1 stop (K11 ECOAST), ~10 min incl. walk, no transfer. DiDi ~8 min, ¥14. Direct metro.
notes: This is the K11 one you saw (K11's 'Pet Show' is an art exhibition, not live animals). 12yo: ideal; 4yo: fine with supervision (adult cats can be jumpy). Opened Apr 2025.

name: Puppy Goes to Work Dog Cafe 小狗上班狗咖
desc: Dog cafe with 70+ dogs of many breeds to pet, walk and play with; free drink and pet treats included, very social and lively.

price.note: weekday unlimited; weekend ¥108/2h; incl. drink + treats

commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 桃园 (欢乐颂/金鸡路), ~30 min, no transfer. DiDi ~18-22 min, ¥28-35. DiDi faster; Line 12 steadier at peak.
notes: 12yo: loves it (lots of dogs); 4yo: only if comfortable with many energetic dogs, supervise. Walk-in friendly. Trip.com well-reviewed.

name: Shangshan Cat & Dog Cafe 上山狗狗事务所 (南山店)
address: Rm 1202, Block B, Min Bldg, Wenxin Rd, Nanshan
desc: Upstairs walk-in cat-and-dog cafe where you pet and play with friendly dogs and cats over a drink; relaxed living-room style space.

price.note: approx incl. drink; verify on Dianping

commute: Direct Line 2 from Sea World to 海月 (文心路/后海), ~24 min, no transfer. DiDi ~15 min, ¥22. DiDi faster; metro cheaper.
notes: 12yo: great; 4yo: okay with supervision (dogs can be lively). Both cats and dogs in one stop. Walk-in friendly.

name: SUPAW HUB (Raffles City) 深圳来福士店
address: Shop 19A, L2, Raffles City, 2163 Nanhai Ave, Nanshan
desc: Nature-themed pet-lifestyle complex blending a cafe, retail and a pet play area, a calm, design-led 'urban wilderness' space for relaxed animal interaction.
commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 南油 (Exit E, Raffles), ~20-25 min, no transfer. DiDi ~15 min, ¥22. DiDi saves time; direct metro also fine.
notes: 12yo: pleasant, calm; 4yo: fine, gentle. Runs a membership/community model, call ahead, less of a pure walk-in petting venue.

name: Yunqi Shrimp-Fishing 云栖钓虾营地 (南油店)
address: Dongbin Rd, Nanshan (near Nanyou / Dongjiaotou Metro)
desc: Indoor air-conditioned shrimp-fishing bar: kids drop a baited rod, pull up live prawns, and staff white-boil or salt-pepper cook your catch on the spot. Genuine catch-and-cook.
commute: ~10 min from Shekou.
notes: Catch-your-own shrimp (not fish, no real catch-your-fish restaurant exists in range). 12yo fishes solo; a 4yo needs a parent to handle the rod, but loves it. Indoor, supervised.

name: SKG Shrimp-Fishing Hall SKG娱乐钓虾馆
address: Yilida Bldg Block B, Nanshan Ave, Nanshan (Line 11)
desc: Indoor shrimp-fishing parlour where children hook live prawns one rod each and staff boil/grill the catch to eat right there. Casual, snack-bar style with drinks and games.
commute: ~12-15 min from Shekou.
notes: Catch-and-cook shrimp. 12yo fishes independently; 4yo participates with help. Indoor and supervised.

name: Qingqing World eco-park 青青世界
address: 1 Qingqing Rd, Yueliangwan, Nanshan (foot of Dananshan)
desc: AAAA eco theme-park where kids fish a stocked pond, scoop goldfish, catch loaches by hand and do pottery/clay DIY, with an on-site farm restaurant for meals.

price.note: park entry; fishing+park combo ¥128 incl. 4h fishing

commute: ~18-20 min from Shekou.
notes: Best all-round interactive kid venue: excellent for both 4yo and 12yo. Note: fishing is recreational (pay by weight to take the fish), the restaurant does not cook your catch. No entry after 17:30.

name: Pipi Shrimp-Fishing Hall 皮皮钓虾馆
address: 326-109 Fanshen Rd, Bao'an (near Fanshen Metro L5)
desc: Indoor pond stocked daily with fresh prawns; one rod per person, kids catch their own and staff white-boil them on the spot to eat fresh (现钓现吃).
commute: ~40 min from Shekou (Bao'an, edge of range).
notes: Genuine catch-and-cook shrimp (现场白灼加工). Easy for a 4yo with help, engaging for a 12yo. Metro-accessible, indoor.

name: Tairyo Teppanyaki 大渔铁板烧 (海上世界店)
address: L2-208, Bldg 1 Zone B, Sea World, 1128 Wanghai Rd, Shekou
desc: All-you-can-eat Japanese teppanyaki: chefs cook on the iron plate right in front of you with the classic flair show (sizzle, flips, the onion-ring 'volcano'), grilling salmon, foie gras, scallops, tiger prawns, even Häagen-Dazs.

price.note: per person AYCE incl. drinks; also à la carte

commute: ~7 min from Shekou.
notes: Best kid spectacle: hibachi-style flips, fire and the onion volcano, and AYCE keeps everyone fed. Dianping ~4.3-4.5. Cooking is at the teppan in front of diners.

name: Hemu Omakase Teppanyaki 禾木鲸生Omakase铁板烧 (海上世界店)
address: Tower 17 Phase 1, Bitao Garden, Taizi Rd, Shekou
desc: Omakase-style teppanyaki, sit at the griddle counter (板前) and the chef cooks each course in front of you (A5 wagyu, foie gras, seafood), explaining each dish.
commute: ~6 min from Shekou.
notes: Refined counter cooking rather than a big flip-show. 12yo enjoys the close-up grilling/torching; a 4yo may need patience. Dianping 4.8 (new).

name: Wangshunge (tableside duck) 旺顺阁鱼头泡饼 (海上世界店)
address: Bldg 1-305, Sea World Stern Plaza, 1128 Wanghai Rd, Shekou
desc: Northern-Chinese restaurant known for tableside Peking-duck carving plus a live kuaiban (快板 clapper) performance, staff wheel a cart over and slice the roast duck in front of you.
commute: ~7 min from Shekou.
notes: Great non-Japanese 'show' for both kids: tableside carving + a clapper act. Dianping ~4.5, sea-view rooms.

name: KYOKU Teppanyaki 泓松京都KYOKU铁板烧 (K11 ECOAST)
address: K11 ECOAST waterfront food market, Taizi Bay, Shekou
desc: A Kyoto-themed Japanese food 'market' of open-front 板前 stalls; the teppanyaki station has the chef grilling at the counter an arm's length away, alongside tempura, sushi and yakitori.
commute: ~12 min from Shekou.
notes: Lively Kyoto-market vibe with several open kitchens to watch, fun for kids, sea views. New (K11 ECOAST, Apr 2025).

name: OPEN live-station buffet (Hilton Shekou) 希尔顿OPEN餐厅
address: 1F, Hilton Shenzhen Shekou Nanhai, 1177 Wanghai Rd
desc: All-day buffet built around interactive live-cooking stations: watch chefs grill, carve and cook regional Asian dishes, western grills and fresh seafood at open counters.
commute: ~8 min from Shekou.
notes: Kids can walk up and watch chefs at the live stations and pick what they want, lower-key than a flip-show but very kid-flexible. Tripadvisor well-rated.

name: Cream Story Italian Gelato 可丽牧手工冰淇淋 (南海意库)
address: 3-3/3-4 Xinghua 1st Rd, Nanhai Yiku, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: Artisanal Italian gelato made fresh daily with 24+ rotating flavours (HK fresh milk, imported pistachio/hazelnut). One of Shenzhen's earliest boutique gelaterias; free tasting before you buy.
commute: ~6-7 min walk (~450 m) from the apartment, just past Sea World toward Nanhai Yiku.
notes: Cream Story brand is consistently Dianping 4.5+; this Yiku branch is newer.

name: Yuyue Sichuan Cuisine 渝月川菜 (海上世界·环船广场)
address: Huanchuan Plaza F2, Nüwa Rd, Sea World, Shekou
desc: Polished Chongqing-Sichuan restaurant on Dianping's 必吃榜 (Must-Eat List): oil-poured boiling fish (油泼沸腾鱼), saliva chicken, 水煮牛肉 and 辣子鸡. Good for group dinners.
commute: ~7 min walk (~500 m) from the apartment.
notes: Dianping 必吃榜 Sichuan, sibling branches ~4.4-4.5.

name: TanYu Grilled Fish 探鱼烤鱼 (海上世界店)
address: Sea World F1, Huanchuan Plaza, Shekou
desc: Well-known Sichuan-style grilled-fish (烤鱼) chain on Dianping's 必吃榜: signature fresh green-pepper numbing grilled fish (鲜青椒爽麻烤鱼) and 毛血旺烤鱼. A spicy, shareable meal.
commute: ~7 min walk (~480 m) from the apartment.
notes: Dianping 必吃榜 grilled-fish chain.

name: Dragon Sake Bar Izakaya 龙酒场居酒屋
address: Sea World Zone A, L1, 1128 Wanghai Rd, Shekou
desc: Low-key Japanese izakaya run by a Nagoya native: well-regarded salmon sashimi, hand-rolled sushi, tempura and Kansai sukiyaki, with a deep sake list. The most solid walkable sushi/日料 at Sea World.
commute: ~6 min walk from the apartment.
notes: Dianping 4.4 / TripAdvisor 4.0. Izakaya style, not an omakase counter.

name: Hongsong Kyoto Japanese Market 泓松京都板前料理 (K11)
address: K11 ECOAST South Bldg L5 504-508, Taizi Bay, Shekou
desc: Kyoto-style Japanese 'market' of nine counter-cooking (板前) stalls in the new K11 ECOAST: sushi, sashimi, tempura, teppanyaki and skewers prepared in front of you. The upscale walkable sushi option.
commute: ~15 min walk from the apartment (edge of range).
notes: New (K11 ECOAST, opened Apr 2025); reviews still settling.

name: Sumo Ramen 相扑拉面 (K11)
address: K11 ECOAST South Bldg L5 SL516, Taizi Bay, Shekou
desc: Hakata-style tonkotsu ramen specialist in K11 ECOAST; signature spicy sesame tonkotsu (招牌辣麻豚骨拉面) with a rich pork-bone broth. The nearest genuine ramen-ya to the apartment.
commute: ~15 min walk from the apartment (edge of range).
notes: New K11 venue. Older fallback: 歌志軒 Komachi Nagoya ramen, 招商花园城 (~1.5 km). Caveat: OpenRice now lists this K11 unit as closed (已結業); verify before going.

name: Latina Brazilian Steakhouse 拉蒂娜巴西烤肉 (海上世界店)
address: Sea World Zone C, 001-C004, 1128 Wanghai Rd, Shekou
desc: Genuine Brazilian churrasco rodízio: skewers of beef, pork and seafood carved tableside plus a full Brazilian buffet (salads, pastas, desserts). Terrace tables catch the Sea World fountain show. The one real Brazilian BBQ in walking distance.
commute: ~6 min walk from the apartment.
notes: Dianping 4.3 (just under 4.5 but the only walkable Brazilian BBQ). China churrasco pioneer since 1998.

name: Lao Wan Hui Shaanxi Noodles 老碗会 (海上世界汇港)
address: Hui Gang Phase 1, L1-16/17, 1 Gongye 3rd Rd, Sea World, Shekou
desc: Shaanxi big-bowl (老碗) noodle house: biangbiang面, oil-splashed 油泼面, 臊子面 and 肉夹馍. Right inside the Sea World Hui Gang mall, the closest Shaanxi option to the apartment.
commute: ~1-2 min walk (~80 m), inside Sea World Hui Gang.
notes: Amap 4.5.

name: Tan Qin Ji Shaanxi Noodles 探秦记陕西面馆 (花果山店)
address: Huaguoshan Bldg A/B, Unit 103, Shekou, Nanshan
desc: A dedicated 陕西面馆 specializing in Shaanxi noodles (biangbiang / 油泼面) and 肉夹馍. The nearest purpose-built Shaanxi noodle restaurant, a bit inland from Sea World.
commute: ~15 min walk (~1.2 km) from the apartment.
notes: Amap 4.3. Coords approximate.

name: Nanyou Clothing & Luggage Wholesale 南油服装批发 (飞马城)
address: Tian'an Bldg 112, 2087 Nanhai Ave / Feima City 204, Xiangnan Rd, Nanyou, Nanshan
desc: Sprawling foreign-trade (外贸原单/尾货) clothing wholesale district of 10+ buildings, Chinese brands and Euro/Japanese/Korean knockoff-style fashion, plus a dedicated luggage (箱包) section. Wholesale prices, single pieces OK, gritty market vibe.
commute: Direct Line 12 from Sea World to 南油 (Exit E, just 3 stops), ~20 min. DiDi ~13 min, ¥18-25. Very close, take the direct metro.
notes: This is the clothes + luggage market you recall (飞马城204栋 服装箱包批发). Bargaining expected; busiest afternoons. Not a mall.

name: Women's World 女人世界 (华强北)
desc: Multi-floor warren of small stalls aimed at women: clothing, lingerie, sleepwear, leather goods and cosmetics, mid-to-low budget fast fashion (~¥30-80). Crowded, haggle-friendly.
commute: Direct Line 2 from Sea World to 华强北 (Exit B), ~55 min, no transfer. DiDi ~35 min, ¥55-75. Metro beats traffic.
notes: Cheap Chinese-brand women's clothing; bargaining expected.

name: Dongmen Baima Clothing City 东门白马服装城
desc: Shenzhen's largest and most influential clothing wholesale/retail market, 6 floors (B1-5F): trendy women's wear (2F), menswear (3-4F), foreign-trade goods (5F). Very cheap, huge variety, busy old-street vibe.
commute: Metro ~62 min: Line 2 to 大剧院, change Line 1 to 老街 (1 stop, Dongmen). DiDi ~45 min, ¥80-100 (Dongmen jams). Recommend metro.
notes: Cheap Chinese-brand + foreign-trade clothes; bargaining expected. Cheapest early morning.

name: Jinya Toy & Stationery Wholesale Market 金雅文具玩具批发市场
address: Guimiao W Rd, Fuhaiyuan 1-2F, Nanshan (near Daxin Metro)
desc: Nanshan's only large dedicated toy-and-stationery wholesale market (~20,000 m²): rows of stalls with cheap toys, plush, blind-boxes, model/hobby kits, party goods and stationery at wholesale prices. Gritty arcade vibe, bargaining expected.
commute: Metro ~38 min: Line 12 to 桃园, change Line 1 to 大新 (Exit C2, 12-15 min walk). DiDi ~20 min, ¥30-38. Close, DiDi is quicker.
notes: Dedicated toy/stationery wholesale market, not electronics, not a mall. Confirm exact pin on Amap (金雅文具玩具批发市场).

name: Sungang Toy & Gift Wholesale Market 笋岗文具玩具礼品批发市场
address: 2033 Bao'an N Rd, Sungang, Luohu (near Sungang Metro, Line 7)
desc: Shenzhen's largest, best-known toy/stationery/gift wholesale market: a 4-storey, ~10,000 m² building with 300+ stalls of cheap toys, plush, blind-boxes (Labubu/Pop Mart-style, Crayon Shin-chan, etc.), figurines, party supplies and stationery. Classic wholesale-trade vibe, haggling common.
commute: Metro ~70 min: Line 2 to 华强北, change Line 7 to 笋岗 (Exit A). DiDi ~40 min, ¥80-95 (Luohu jams). Recommend metro, punctual and cheaper.
notes: Dedicated toy/gift wholesale market (not electronics, not a mall). The big blind-box/plush option for the kids.

name: Shenzhen Bay Sightseeing Cruise 'Greater Bay Area No.1' 海上看深圳·大湾区一号
address: Shekou Cruise Center, 1 Haiyun Rd, Nanshan
desc: A ~100-min loop on the hybrid catamaran 'Greater Bay Area No.1': Shekou port, the container terminal, the Shenzhen Bay Bridge and back, with open-air top-deck views of the bay and skyline. Ticket includes an afternoon-tea snack set; comfortable for kids.

price.note: adult ¥150 online (¥200 gate); child <6 ¥75; under 1 free; ~100 min

commute: ~5 min, Shekou Cruise Center is right by the apartment.
notes: Sailings 16:00 (day) and 19:00 (sunset/skyline). Trip.com 4.6/5. Collect tickets 30 min before; real-name booking; weather-dependent. Book via Shekou Cruise WeChat (cmskchp) or Ctrip.

name: HK-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge Sea Cruise 港珠澳大桥海上游
address: Shekou Cruise Center, 1 Haiyun Rd, Nanshan
desc: A ~3-hour open-sea sightseeing cruise on the same 'Greater Bay Area No.1' vessel out to the HK-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge and back, with on-board entertainment. Some departures are sunset + buffet-dinner sailings with music/magic shows.

price.note: from ~¥198 adult (¥218 standard; child ¥109); dinner dates ¥318-398; ~3h

commute: ~5 min from the apartment.
notes: Roughly every 2h, 08:00-18:00 (varies); dinner/show sailings ~18:00. Same 4.6/5 operator. ID required; book via Ctrip or Shekou Cruise WeChat. (A multi-day luxury ship, 招商伊敦号, is also home-ported here.)

name: Lianghang Sea-Fishing Club 领航海钓 (Bao'an airport pier)
address: Airport/Fuyong Ferry Terminal, Bao'an (boats fish Qianhai/Shekou/estuary waters)
desc: The nearest operator to Shekou for genuine 出海船钓: boat and rock fishing into the Qianhai/Shekou/Pearl-estuary waters, plus all-day dock fishing on the pier. More an anglers' club than a polished tourist boat.

price.note: boat fishing ¥200-1000/person by distance; dock fishing ¥188/day

commute: Metro+ ~70 min, ¥8 (Line 12 → 福永 → Line 11 to 机场, then taxi/bus ~10 min to the pier). DiDi direct ~40 min, ¥70-90. DiDi if catching a boat.
notes: No charter departs Shekou pier itself; this is the nearest that fishes Shekou/Qianhai waters. Bait, buckets, ice provided; doable with kids. Book via Dianping/Meituan (search 海钓).

name: Nan'ao Fish-Raft Sea Fishing 南澳鱼排海钓 (Dapeng)
address: Xiexia Pier / Nan'ao Fishing Harbour, Dapeng
desc: The most family/beginner-friendly pick: a speedboat ferries you to a stable floating fish-raft where kids fish for sea bass and small grouper (beginner level), and the restaurant cooks your catch. You can also charter a 4-person speedboat for open-water 海钓.
commute: Dapeng has no metro: metro+bus is ~3 h (very inconvenient). Best by DiDi/charter direct, ~75 km, 1.5-2 h, ¥200-280 (charter ¥300+).
notes: Best for kids: stable raft, beginner level, catch likely; rods rentable ¥30 + bait ¥20; crab/shrimp cages ¥30. Bring seasickness meds. Book via Dianping/Meituan (海钓 + date).

name: Seven-Star Bay Yacht Fishing 七星湾游艇海钓 (Dapeng)
desc: A premium, fully-equipped option: charter a yacht/fishing boat with rods and bait provided, running ~3-hour trips to fish-rich spots like Sanmen Island and Yangmeikeng. Comfortable and well-organised for families.

price.note: yacht/boat charter per boat, ~3h half-day; call club to quote

commute: Dapeng has no metro: metro+bus ~2.5-3 h. Best by DiDi/charter direct to Dongshan, ~65 km, 1.5-2 h, ¥180-250.
notes: Rods and bait supplied; calm, organised, good for a family group (charter pricing). Half-day ~13:30 departure. Contact Seven-Star Bay marina to book.

name: Da Shi Xiong Shaanxi Noodles 大师兄手工面·西北菜 (海岸城)
address: B1, Coastal City Mall, 33 Wenxin 5th Rd, Houhai, Nanshan
desc: A popular dedicated Shaanxi/Northwest hand-noodle 'queue-king': oil-splashed biangbiang面, 老潼关肉夹馍, 陕西凉皮, 岐山臊子面, plus lamb skewers. The best Shaanxi-noodle specialist a short hop from Shekou.
commute: ~1 metro hop: Line 2 from Sea World to 后海 + ~10 min walk (~3.3 km). DiDi ~15 min, ¥25.
notes: Rating couldn't be machine-verified (Dianping blocks bots); realistically ~4.3-4.4, not confirmed above Lao Wan Hui's 4.5. Strongest longer-walk Shaanxi option.
